title: vandellia cordifolia
intro: Vandellia cordifolia, also known as "Kundru" or "Kunduri," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects, which help to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Vandellia cordifolia include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as being used to manage conditions like fever, cough, and skin problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders, which are consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
